title: Post-commit git hook (diff-scoped, soft, non-blocking)
description:
FIRST: 'git merge main --no-edit'; verify T1's surfaced cursor + newly_stale exist.

Add a kb.py path (e.g. 'kb.py post-commit') that, scoped to the LAST commit's diff (HEAD~1..HEAD), reports NEWLY-stale pages (reuse T1 cursor/newly_stale) and proposes re-synthesis for from-code pages whose covers changed. SOFT: print a concise nudge, ALWAYS exit 0 — never block the commit. A commit touching nothing covered prints nothing.

Install a '.git/hooks/post-commit' in cmd_init: idempotent; if a post-commit hook already exists, append our line or warn rather than clobber; make the hook call the repo's kb.py. (Git hooks aren't shared by clone — that's fine; init installs locally. Note this in output.)

Test (temp git repo):
- init installs .git/hooks/post-commit (executable).
- commit a change drifting a covered page → hook prints the newly-stale nudge (+ from-code → re-synthesize); commit succeeds (exit 0).
- commit touching nothing covered → hook prints nothing, exit 0.
- double init → no duplicate hook line.
notes: 2026-06-15 07:45 - [human] ONBOARDING: git hooks are NEVER inherited by clone (.git/hooks/ is local). So the post-commit install must be local, idempotent, and safely re-runnable (don't clobber an existing post-commit hook). A new collaborator won't get this hook from cloning — it's installed by kb.py init or self-healed at SessionStart (see T3).
acceptance_criteria: init installs an idempotent .git/hooks/post-commit; on a drift-causing commit it prints the newly-stale (diff-scoped) nudge + from-code re-synth, always exit 0; no-op when nothing covered changed; never blocks
closed_reason: Completed: kb.py post-commit (HEAD~1..HEAD diff-scoped newly-stale via cursor, from-code→re-synth/canonical→review, always exit 0); install_post_commit_hook in cmd_init (worktree-aware, non-clobbering, idempotent). Verified: fires on drift commit, silent on unrelated, dedup, single line on double-init.
